app_description: Plan and run reproducible protein-interaction predictions.
language_prompt: Choose a language / 请选择语言 / 言語を選択してください [1 中文, 2 English, 3 日本語]:
invalid_language: Please enter 1, 2, 3, zh-CN, en, or ja.
interactive_intro: Describe two proteins and PPI Scout will create a reproducible job plan.
input_hint: You may enter a protein name, a FASTA path, or an amino-acid sequence.
protein_ab_guidance: Label convention: A = the known receptor, pocket, or reference protein; B = the candidate partner or likely motif-bearing protein. In the public Atg8/Atg19 example, use A=Atg8 and B=Atg19. A/B are input labels only: they do not prove interaction direction and are not automatically bait/prey.
protein_a_guidance: Enter protein A so the program can define its first chain. Use a name, one sequence, or a FASTA path, for example Atg8 or atg8.fasta; this cannot be blank. Common confusion: A does not mean larger or more important, and a name alone is not enough for a local scan or prediction.
protein_b_guidance: Enter protein B, the candidate partner to compare with A; for motif projects this is usually the possible motif-bearing protein. For example, use Atg19 or atg19.fasta; this cannot be blank. B is only a label: it does not mean binding is established or that B is the experimental prey.
organism_guidance: Enter the organism or strain to distinguish same-named proteins from different species. Example: Saccharomyces cerevisiae S288c. Blank is allowed and records an unknown value, but do not leave it blank when relying on protein names if you can avoid it. Do not enter a protein or project name here.
mode_guidance: Choose how the proteins should be represented when planning full-length, domain, or motif-peptide work. Use auto unless another representation is already justified; blank is allowed and defaults to auto. This is a computational route, not a biological conclusion, and motif-peptide does not prove motif binding.
job_created: Job plan created. The complete JSON is printed next; save it with `ppi-scout > job.json` before a dry-run or result view, then use `ppi-scout visualize job.json`. Printed output alone is not a completed prediction.
yaml_fallback: PyYAML is not installed; emitting JSON instead.
core_unavailable: The local execution backend is not available yet. Install the full PPI Scout package or use --dry-run.
sequence_required: An amino-acid sequence or FASTA file is required.
motif_not_found: The motif sequence was not found uniquely in the parent sequence.
scan_guidance: Decide whether to enable the local AIM/LIR sequence scan. It lists [W/F/Y]xx[L/I/V] candidates for optional peptide design; for example, enter yes to inspect Atg19. Blank is allowed and the default is off. Common confusion: this is a rule-based sequence screen, not a binding prediction, and it selects nothing automatically.
scan_owner_guidance: Choose whether to scan protein A or B because the program needs the motif-bearing sequence. In the public Atg8/Atg19 example choose B (Atg19); blank is allowed and defaults to B. A/B remain labels and do not prove direction, receptor status, or bait/prey roles.
scan_owner_prompt: Which protein owns the motif? [A/B] (default: B):
scan_select_guidance: Choose which candidates should receive peptide and negative-control designs. Example: aim-002, or aim-001,aim-002 for several. Blank is allowed and designs none. A candidate's score and rank are sequence priorities only; they do not prove exposure, interaction, or binding.
scan_select_prompt: Candidate IDs to design, comma-separated (blank: design none):
scan_candidates_note: sequence candidates only; not binding claims
scan_candidate_features: score features: flank D/E={acidic}, flank S/T={st}; core matched the canonical pattern only
scan_warning_truncated: the flank was truncated by a sequence terminus
scan_warning_unknown: the flank contains unknown residue X
scan_warning_tied: multiple candidates share this score and rank
scan_warning_review: this candidate has an additional sequence warning to review
scan_global_caveat: This is a sequence screen only; it cannot establish motif accessibility, disorder, interaction, or binding.
scan_sequence_required: Motif scanning requires an amino-acid sequence or FASTA file for the selected protein.
visualize_next_step: Double-click this HTML file in your file manager; no local server or network connection is required.
auto_visualization_complete: Run status: {run_status}. The local result page was generated automatically: {path}. Double-click it in your file manager.
auto_visualization_failed: Run status remains {run_status}; only the local result page failed: {error}. This does not change the Boltz outcome. Retry later with: {recovery}
auto_visualization_metadata_failed: The result-page state could not be written back to status.json: {error}. The original run status shown in the terminal remains valid; save this terminal output.
